reasoning: In April 2025, HHS Secretary RFK Jr. explicitly directed the CDC to stop recommending the addition of fluoride to community drinking water. The CDC responded by stating it would re-examine the evidence, and as of May 2025, a task force was being assembled to review the policy and issue new recommendations. Given RFK Jr.'s strong, long-standing opposition to water fluoridation and his direct authority over the CDC, it is highly probable that the CDC will comply quickly. Even if the task force has not finalized a new permanent standard by July 1, 2025, standard administrative procedure for policy reversals dictates that the old recommendation (0.7 mg/L) will be suspended, removed from the active webpage, or the page will be taken down or marked "under review" in the interim. Any of these actions would resolve the question as No. The 3-month window from the April announcement to July 1 provides ample time for this straightforward website update. There remains only a small chance that bureaucratic inertia or regulatory hurdles delay the modification of the webpage past July 1.
